title: Fibonacci Number
description:
The Fibonacci numbers form a sequence where each number is the sum of the two
preceding ones, starting from `F(0) = 0` and `F(1) = 1`. Given `n`, return
`F(n)`.

solutions.summary: Keep the last two values and roll forward.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Loop up to n
steps.explanation: Advance the pair once per step from 2 to `n`.

steps.title: Roll the pair forward
steps.explanation: Each step the new value is `a + b`; shift the window.

solutions.summary: Apply the definition directly (exponential, for contrast).

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Recurse on the definition
steps.explanation: `F(n)` is the sum of the two previous Fibonacci numbers.
